content.problemStatement:
You are given two positive integers x,yx, yx,y.

Find the smallest positive integer zzz such that zzz contains xxx as a prefix (in base 10) and zzz is divisible by yyy.

If the smallest such z>1018z > 10^{18}z>1018, print -1.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers x,yx, yx,y.


Output Format
For each test case, output an integer zzz corresponding to the answer of the problem. If z>1018z > 10^{18}z>1018, output -1 instead.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two space-separated integers x and y.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the smallest positive integer z such that z contains x as a prefix and z is divisible by y. If z > 10^18, output -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the smallest number that contains 2 as a prefix and is divisible by 3 is 21. For the second test case, the smallest number that contains 12 as a prefix and is divisible by 5 is 120.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a simple iterative approach. For each test case, we start with the given prefix x and keep appending digits to it until we find a number that is divisible by y. We can use the modulo operator to check for divisibility.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * 10^18) — in the worst case, we might need to iterate up to 10^18 times for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we only need a constant amount of space to store the input and output values.
